The search strategy encompassed the following keywords: "deep brain stimulation", "Parkinson's disease", "motor pathway", and "human", from January 1, 2012, to December 1, 2022.

CONCLUSION: This study has provided a new perspective through bibliometric analysis of the deep brain stimulation therapy for treating patients with PD, which can shed light on future research to advance our comprehension of this particular field of study.

grouped_hits.title: Treatment of Motor Symptoms of Parkinson's Disease.
matched_sentences: The most effective pharmacologic intervention used to treat motor symptoms in Parkinson's disease is levodopa, which is available in various formulations, including newer continuous subcutaneous infusions.

With disease progression, pharmacologic interventions alone may not suffice to manage motor symptoms, making it necessary to consider device-aided therapies (eg, levodopa-carbidopa intestinal gel infusion, continuous subcutaneous infusions of apomorphine, levodopa, or foslevodopa) or invasive surgical techniques (eg, deep brain stimulation or MRI-guided high-frequency focused ultrasound).

grouped_hits.title: Adaptive Deep Brain Stimulation for sleep stage targeting in Parkinson's disease.

matched_sentences: Develop an adaptive Deep Brain Stimulation algorithm that modulates stimulation parameters in response to intracranially classified sleep stages.
